skill.name: community-marketing
skill.description: Create community strategies, launch plans, engagement programs, ambassador systems, and health audits for product-led communities.
skill.summary: Plan and improve online communities that support product growth, retention, and member value.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, network reconnaissance, and Ruby or shell backtick execution, but these are false positives from marketing text, platform names, and Markdown formatting. No prompt injection, malicious commands, network calls, or cryptographic code were found. The only real concern is a benign instruction to read local product marketing context files when present.

low_findings.title: Local Context File Read Instruction
low_findings.description: The skill asks the agent to check for and read product marketing context files before asking questions. This is a limited filesystem access pattern used for user context, with no evidence of exfiltration or hidden processing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction to read local context files is explicit. The risk remains low because paths are specific, user-relevant, and not paired with network transmission or command execution.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations contain community marketing guidance, platform names, evaluation prompts, or ordinary prose. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, password hashing, signing, encryption, or cryptographic API use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of every cited line found only community strategy content and evaluation text. There is no code or cryptographic operation in either scanned file.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported backtick locations are Markdown inline code around filenames and a fenced text diagram. They do not invoke Ruby, a shell, or any executable command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is Markdown documentation, not Ruby source. The fenced block is a community flywheel diagram, and line 15 only names possible context files.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Match Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The reported network reconnaissance location is a marketing value bullet about exclusive knowledge or early access. No evidence found of host discovery, port scanning, DNS probing, IP enumeration, or network tooling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain marketing guidance. It contains no network target, command, URL, scanning instruction, or data collection behavior.

content.value_statement: Many teams launch communities without a clear member promise or operating rhythm. This skill turns community goals into platform choices, onboarding flows, rituals, ambassador plans, and health audits.
actual_capabilities: Recommends community platforms based on audience, goals, and operating constraints.
Builds launch plans for new communities, including founding member recruitment and seeded discussions.
Designs member journeys with welcome posts, onboarding prompts, and early engagement paths.
Creates ambassador and advocate program briefs with criteria, benefits, assets, and tracking plans.
Audits community health using activity, reply, churn, and non-staff content metrics.
Suggests recurring rituals, channel structures, and support deflection workflows.
limitations: Does not connect directly to Discord, Slack, Reddit, Circle, or analytics tools.
Requires user-provided context for accurate audience, product, and platform recommendations.
Cannot verify live community metrics unless the user supplies the data.
Does not replace legal, moderation, privacy, or trust and safety review.

use_cases.title: Launch a B2B SaaS Community
use_cases.description: Create a platform recommendation, founding member plan, initial channel structure, and first 90 days of engagement.

use_cases.title: Revive a Quiet Customer Community
use_cases.description: Diagnose where members drop off and define rituals, onboarding fixes, and priority metrics for recovery.

use_cases.title: Formalize an Advocate Program
use_cases.description: Turn helpful members into recognized advocates with clear criteria, benefits, enablement assets, and measurement.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Community Platform
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a community platform for [product]. Our audience is [audience], our goal is [goal], and we can spend [hours] per week managing it.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Community Launch
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 90-day launch plan for a new [platform] community for [audience]. Include founding member recruitment, seeded posts, channels, and weekly rituals.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Community Health
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit our community health. We have [member count], [active member count], [new member post rate], [reply rate], and [top concerns]. Give the top three fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Ambassador Program
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an ambassador program for our community. Include candidate criteria, outreach, benefits, enablement assets, governance, and measurement.

output_examples.input: We are a B2B SaaS company choosing between Discord and Slack.
output_examples.output: A recommendation that compares Slack, Discord, and Circle against buyer context, searchability, onboarding friction, and management effort.

output_examples.input: Our 40-member community has only two active posters.
output: A diagnosis of new member activation problems.
A practical onboarding path with personal welcomes, seeded posts, and weekly rituals.
A focus plan for power users and first-week posting behavior.

output_examples.input: We want to reduce support tickets through the community.
output_examples.output: A support deflection plan with a knowledge base loop, peer recognition, unanswered-question thresholds, and product feedback follow-up.

best_practices: Provide product, audience, stage, goal, and available team capacity before asking for a strategy.
Share current metrics when requesting an audit, including active members, reply rates, and new member posting.
Ask for concrete deliverables such as launch plans, channel maps, calendars, or ambassador briefs.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic community advice without defining the member identity or business goal.
Do not treat member count as success without engagement, reply, and retention signals.
Do not use the skill to automate moderation decisions without human review.

faq.question: What platforms does this skill support?
faq.answer: It covers Discord, Slack, Circle, Reddit, Facebook Groups, forums, and similar community platforms.

faq.question: Can it connect to my live community data?
faq.answer: No. It can analyze metrics and examples that you provide, but it does not connect to live platforms.

faq.question: Does it help with community launch strategy?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create launch plans, founding member tactics, seeded content, channel structures, and early rituals.

faq.question: Can it build an ambassador program?
faq.answer: Yes. It can define selection criteria, outreach, benefits, enablement assets, and measurement for advocates.

faq.question: Is it useful for support communities?
faq.answer: Yes. It can plan peer support workflows, knowledge base loops, helper recognition, and sentiment monitoring.

faq.question: What should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide the product, audience, community stage, platform, business goal, current metrics, and team capacity.
